business.headline: Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as IT stocks drag; Q1 earnings in focusBenchmark indices erased opening gains on Tuesday as IT stocks pulled the market lower. Attention is now shifting to June-quarter earnings, with analysts watching financials, healthcare and power.

Gold prices see biggest monthly drop in 18 years. Good time to buy?Gold prices have slid sharply from record highs as investors weigh higher US rates and a stronger dollar. The correction has reopened the buying debate, with upcoming US data likely to shape the next move.

Microsoft loses $570 billion in a month. Is Wall Street losing faith in AI boom?Microsoft has lost more than $570 billion in market value this month, marking its worst monthly performance since the dot-com era. Investors are increasingly questioning whether the software giant's massive AI investments will generate enough profits to justify the spending.

Japan's yen falls to 40-year low against US dollarThe yen fell to its weakest level against the US dollar since 1986, reviving expectations of fresh market intervention by Tokyo. The slide reflects the widening US-Japan rate gap and keeps pressure on Japan's economy and global investors.

Old vs new tax regime: How standard deduction works and who benefitsAs the ITR filing season gathers pace, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series breaks down key tax benefits. In the first part, we explain the standard deduction and how it works under the old and new tax regimes.

HDFC Bank appoints ex-poll body chief Rajiv Kumar as chairman; RBI nod awaitedHDFC Bank has appointed former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as its new chairman, subject to RBI approval, replacing Atanu Chakraborty.
Yen sinks to 40-year low, dollar slips but stays near 13-month highThe Japanese yen weakened to its lowest levels against the US currency since 1986.

Govt to lift petrol and diesel sales curbs from July 1 as supply concerns easeThe precautionary measures were introduced to protect domestic fuel supplies at a time when uncertainty in the global energy market was increasing.

From ITR deadlines to passport fees: 5 financial changes from July 1 you must knowWhether you're planning to file your ITR, apply for a passport or simply keep track of your finances, July 1 brings a few important changes you shouldn't ignore. Here are five key financial updates that could matter to you. Which ones will affect you the most?

Markets snap winning streak as weak monsoon worries trigger profit bookingThe BSE Sensex fell 372.10 points, or 0.48%, to close at 76,728.37, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 109.75 points, or 0.46%, to settle at 23,946.25.

Amazon, Flipkart quick commerce push dents Blinkit and SwiggyAmazon and Flipkart are rapidly expanding their quick commerce networks across India. The push has deepened investor concerns over profitability, valuations and a prolonged price war.

EPFO 3.0: 5 PF mistakes that could delay your transfer when switching jobsChanging jobs comes with plenty of paperwork, and transferring your PF is one task you shouldn't ignore. While EPFO 3.0 promises a faster and smoother process, a few simple mistakes could still delay your money. Are you making any of them?

Gold price has crashed nearly Rs 50,000 from its peak. Will prices fall further?Gold has slipped sharply from its MCX record high as investors reassess the US rate outlook. The fall has given buyers some relief, but volatility remains tied to inflation, oil and Federal Reserve signals.

BAT to slash 9,000 jobs amid shift to smoke-free productsThe move is part of the company's wider plan to reduce costs while investing more in smoke-free products such as vaping devices and nicotine pouches.

Nearly Rs 9,000 crore in unclaimed insurance: How to check if any is yoursNearly Rs 8,974 crore in insurance money remains unclaimed with insurers across India. Old policies, missing nominee details and outdated records could leave rightful dues untouched for years.

Samsung, SK Hynix's $518 billion AI bet: Why the chip giants are expandingAs the AI boom fuels a global race for computing power, Samsung and SK Hynix are making one of their biggest expansion bets yet. Here's why the chip giants are investing $518 billion.

Home sales slip 6%: Why are buyers pulling back across top cities?ANAROCK said housing sales across the top seven cities fell in Q2 2026 even as new launches rose. The data showed a more balanced market as war-linked uncertainty and IT sector concerns weighed on buyers.

One missed EMI, endless harassment: The dark side of digital lending boomDigital lending has made borrowing faster and easier than ever before. Yet behind the promise of instant cash lies a troubling reality: for some borrowers, a single missed EMI can lead to harassment, intimidation and financial distress.

Think your Rs 5 lakh health insurance cover is enough? It may not beA new analysis of more than one lakh policyholders by insurance platform CoverSure has found that a majority of people above the age of 50 have health insurance coverage of less than Rs 5 lakh. Even more worrying, 17% of policyholders with chronic medical conditions also have a sum insured below Rs 5 lakh.

ITR filing 2026: July's biggest income tax deadlines explainedJuly brings six key income tax deadlines, including the 31 July ITR filing cut-off. Missing them can lead to late fees, interest and limits on carrying forward losses.

entertainment.headline: Lock Upp: Ram Kapoor says one can cheat by mistake, Akanksha Chamola shuts him down Ram Kapoor and Akanksha Chamola clashed on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa over whether cheating can ever be a mistake in marriage. The exchange put trust, forgiveness and accountability at the centre of the show's latest talking point.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 11: Shahid's film surpasses O'Romeo's lifetime haul Cocktail 2 continued its steady box office run on Day 11, surpassing the lifetime India nett collection of O'Romeo. Despite a slowdown in its second week, Shahid Kapoor's romantic drama has crossed Rs 84 crore nett in India and Rs 129 crore worldwide.

Samay Raina's old joke on Latent triggers backlash, fans cite biryani row An old India's Got Latent clip shows Samay Raina making a sexual remark after an audience member said his girlfriend left because her father had suffered a minor heart attack. The resurfaced video has drawn criticism.

Director Rahul Ravindran deletes X after death threats aimed at his children Director Rahul Ravindran said he is deleting X after receiving death threats linked to the Ketan Agarwal debate, including those targeting his children. The backlash widened after Chinmayi Sripada defended him, prompting Rahul to step away from the platform.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 4: Akshay's film crosses Rs 100 crore globally Welcome To The Jungle has maintained its strong box office momentum, earning Rs 8.50 crore nett on its first Monday. The Akshay Kumar-led entertainer has now crossed the Rs 100 crore mark worldwide in just four days.

Welcome To The Jungle spoofs Bollywood tropes, but silly gags blunt the impact Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle tries lampooning Bollywood stereotypes as well as moments from the Welcome franchise. The film's slapstick overdose blunts the impact.

Kylie Jenner sued as former chef says intense workload led to her miscarriage Kylie Jenner is facing a third workplace lawsuit after a former private chef alleged that demanding work conditions during her high-risk pregnancy led to a miscarriage.

Maa Inti Baangaram and Tollywood's 16-year wait to break Arundhati's record Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram has crossed Arundhati's lifetime worldwide gross within 10 days. The run has renewed focus on how rarely Tollywood backs solo woman-led commercial films.

Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce's wedding invites carry hidden watermarks Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have reportedly sent traceable invitations for their wedding. The couple are rumoured to get married on July 3.

Is Raghav Juyal dating actor-YouTuber Niharika NM? Cosy photos sparks buzz Actor Raghav Juyal and YouTuber Niharika NM have got the internet guessing after sharing cosy Instagram pictures. Fans are now asking the big question: relationship reveal or a teaser for their upcoming film, Bhai Tera Star Hai?

Elle review: Pretty and heartfelt, but this Legally Blonde prequel lacks OG magicRelease Date:Wed Jul 01, 2026 Prime Video's Elle revisits Elle Woods's teenage years in 1995 Seattle. The prequel has warmth and style, even if it lacks the original's sharp payoff.

Actor Akanksha Chamola breaks down, calls divorce from Gaurav Khanna devastating Actor Akanksha Chamola broke down on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa days after revealing her divorce from Gaurav Khanna. She said sharing the life-changing secret on the show was devastating for her.

Angry Birds Movie 3 trailer sees Red juggle fatherhood and saving the world The trailer for The Angry Birds Movie 3 is finally here, but there's more than another battle with the pigs. Here's what the first look reveals about Red's next adventure.

Sunny Deol wanted to make Damini 2. Here's why it didn't happen Sunny Deol has revealed that he spent years trying to make Damini 2, but the sequel never took off. The actor explains how Ikka became the courtroom drama that finally brought him back to the genre.

Singer Jasmine Sandlas faces backlash over late Hyderabad show, lip-sync claims A concert attendee has accused Jasmine Sandlas of reaching her Hyderabad show more than three hours late and lip-syncing on stage. The viral post has drawn criticism from fans, while the singer and organisers have yet to respond.

What happened to Jasmin Bhasin? Actor reveals reason for hospitalisation Actor Jasmin Bhasin said she was hospitalised in Dubai after being diagnosed with terminal ileitis and a severe infection. She celebrated her birthday with boyfriend Aly Goni in the hospital.

Uorfi Javed denies claims of converting to Hinduism and changing her name Actor Uorfi Javed has rejected claims that she converted and changed her name. The exchange escalated after the actor said she received triggering messages and false remarks about her appearance.

Ikka trailer: Sunny Deol, Akshaye Khanna in a new Ram-Raavan battle for justice The trailer of Ikka shows actor Sunny Deol's lawyer Arjun Mehra defending accused Shauryamann Gaur, played by actor Akshaye Khanna, in an attempted murder case. The film draws parallels to Ram-Raavan's battle for justice.

Kannada actor Krishi Thapanda breaks silence on man's death at her Bengaluru home Actor Krishi Thapanda has broken her silence after businessman Vaishak was found dead at her Bengaluru home. Here's what the actor said as the police investigation continues.

general news.headline: VHP turns its back on Champat Rai amid Ram Mandir theft row: Our role's overThe Vishwa Hindu Parishad's evasive remarks come at a politically sensitive moment as scrutiny intensifies over the diversion of devotees' offerings and the functioning of the trust overseeing one of the country's most significant religious sites.

Will cut off those hands: Pak minister's warning to India over Indus watersAddressing a joint press conference with Climate Change Minister Musadik Malik, Information Minister Attaullah Tarar insisted that the Indus Waters Treaty remained legally binding and could not be suspended, revoked or amended unilaterally.

Monsoon engine revving up: Twin systems to push heavy rain across IndiaTwo low-pressure systems and a shifting monsoon trough are set to strengthen rain across India. The shift could end north India's dry spell while raising flooding and disruption risks in rain-hit areas.

Usernames coming to WhatsApp, CEO Kunal Shah says reserve your name nowWhatsApp will soon let users connect without revealing their phone numbers. CEO Kunal Shah has announced that usernames are coming to the messaging platform and is encouraging users to reserve their preferred names before the feature rolls out more widely later this year.

Japan coach, players bow down to fans in apology as Brazil end World Cup dreamFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu and his players bowed before their fans after Brazil's dramatic 2-1 win ended the Samurai Blue's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign in an emotional Round of 32 farewell.

Wait for name in voter list, cops said: Top journalist's charge in passport rowCaught between a pending electoral appeal before the Appelate Tribunal and a stalled passport renewal, former The Telegraph editor R Rajagopal told IndiaToday.in he is in a "state of limbo" with no clarity on when either process will reach a conclusion.

Woman found dead at homestay; family claims boyfriend beat and drugged herA woman was found dead at a homestay in Karnataka's Chikkaballapur district, while her boyfriend was found unconscious in the same room. Police are investigating the incident from various angles, including murder or suicide.

FIR against cricketer Shashank Singh and retired IPS father for assaulting cookPunjab Kings player Shashank Singh, his retired IPS father and their driver have been booked after their cook alleged assault and abuse in Bhopal. The complaint includes claims of phone seizure and visible injuries, with police now investigating the case.

UP man Ayush Malik, who converted to Islam for lover, returns to HinduismAyush Malik, the Shamli man whose conversion to Islam led to an investigation under Uttar Pradesh's anti-conversion law, has returned to Hinduism, saying he did so voluntarily after considering his family's distress.

Gold prices see biggest monthly drop in 18 years. Good time to buy?Gold prices have slid sharply from record highs as investors weigh higher US rates and a stronger dollar. The correction has reopened the buying debate, with upcoming US data likely to shape the next move.

Not anti-American, wasn't kneeling: Meloni doubles down after Trump G7 photo feudItalian PM Giorgia Meloni rejected US President Donald Trump's claims and denied taking an anti-American line. The clash widened into a diplomatic spat, with Italy stressing sovereignty and cancelling a planned US visit.

Who is Faiyaz Premjee, the man accused in Mumbai's Muharram poison plot?Long before police accused him of plotting to poison Muharram mourners, Faiyaz Premjee had built an online profile of an outspoken critic of Islam. As investigators probe his motive to distribute rodenticides in Ashura procession, here's a look at Premjee's life over the years.

Welcome To The Jungle spoofs Bollywood tropes, but silly gags blunt the impactAkshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle tries lampooning Bollywood stereotypes as well as moments from the Welcome franchise. The film's slapstick overdose blunts the impact.

Paraguay's finest hour: Giants Germany sent packing in Round of 32Paraguay held Germany for 120 minutes before winning 4-3 in the shootout after a 1-1 draw. The result handed Germany their first World Cup shootout defeat and gave Paraguay a landmark victory.

Massive fire breaks out in naphtha pipeline at Bengal refinery, 10 injuredA massive fire erupted at West Bengal's Haldia Refinery after a naphtha pipeline caught fire early Tuesday morning. Visuals from the scene showed thick black smoke billowing into the sky as firefighters battled the blaze.

UP Congress chief Ajay Rai alleges house arrest ahead of Ram Mandir visitThe Uttar Pradesh Congress alleged that its state chief was placed under house arrest at a hotel in Ayodhya ahead of the party's Ram Temple visit, with Ajay Rai terming it a "cowardly attempt" by the BJP to prevent party leaders from offering prayers and accusing that the government's "dictatorship has reached its peak."

Where does a feeling in music live, the song or you? The science of soundThe same song can make one person cry and leave another smiling, and science can explain why. From the physics of a note to dopamine, memories and the strange comfort of sad music, here is how sound becomes joy or sorrow in the brain.

Maa Inti Baangaram and Tollywood's 16-year wait to break Arundhati's recordSamantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram has crossed Arundhati's lifetime worldwide gross within 10 days. The run has renewed focus on how rarely Tollywood backs solo woman-led commercial films.

Siya Goyal's 'lawyer' sues her brother for Rs 10 crore over defamatory remarksAdvocate Aashutosh Srivastava has sent a Rs 10 crore defamation notice to Sahil Goyal, brother of murder accused Siya Goyal, over allegedly defamatory statements against him. The notice demands withdrawal of the allegations, a public apology and a written assurance against repeating them.

Harmanpreet Kaur to captain India as BCCI announces Asian Games squadHarmanpreet Kaur will continue to lead India in T20Is. The BCCI on Tuesday announced a 15-member squad for women's cricket at Asian Games 2026, scheduled to be held in Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, in September 2026.

84% at DU, yet no placement: No companies for humanities, says History graduateA Delhi University History graduate with 84% marks and a place on the Dean's List was allegedly told by her college placement cell that no companies were recruiting humanities students, reigniting debate over whether campus placements continue to sideline humanities graduates.

Microsoft loses $570 billion in a month. Is Wall Street losing faith in AI boom?Microsoft has lost more than $570 billion in market value this month, marking its worst monthly performance since the dot-com era. Investors are increasingly questioning whether the software giant's massive AI investments will generate enough profits to justify the spending.

Delhi man receives less salary, confronts HR on camera. Their reaction is enragingA Delhi-based telecom company employee confronted the HR team in a viral office video over an alleged salary shortfall. The exchange has sparked wider debate online.

Trump says Doha talks with Iran are on, Tehran denies any meeting is scheduledTrump said US-Iran talks would be held in Doha, but Tehran denied any meeting had been scheduled. The conflicting messages have deepened uncertainty around a fragile ceasefire and wider regional de-escalation efforts.

sports.headline: Harmanpreet Kaur to captain India as BCCI announces Asian Games squad Harmanpreet Kaur will continue to lead India in T20Is. The BCCI on Tuesday announced a 15-member squad for women's cricket at Asian Games 2026, scheduled to be held in Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, in September 2026.

FIR against cricketer Shashank Singh and retired IPS father for assaulting cook Punjab Kings player Shashank Singh, his retired IPS father and their driver have been booked after their cook alleged assault and abuse in Bhopal. The complaint includes claims of phone seizure and visible injuries, with police now investigating the case.

Australia eye another final as West Indies plot ambush Australia enter the Women's T20 World Cup semi-final as overwhelming favourites after an unbeaten campaign, but a resilient West Indies side will back its experienced match-winners to spring a knockout upset.

Paraguay celebrates historic World Cup win vs Germany with public holiday Paraguay's stunning World Cup win over Germany has sparked nationwide celebrations, with President Santiago Pena declaring a public holiday to mark La Albirroja's historic Round of 16 qualification.

Gakpo in tears, scoring days after unborn son's death, teammates rally around him FIFA World Cup 2026: Cody Gakpo scored for the Netherlands against Morocco in the Round of 32 game, days after his partner announced the loss of their unborn son. The goal sparked an emotional response and drew support from teammates and fans.

Morocco 3-2 Netherlands in penalties: Disastrous Dutch crash out of World Cup Netherlands (NED) vs Morocco (MAR) live, FIFA World Cup 2026: A disastrous penalty shootout sees Netherlands crash out of the FIFA World Cup. 3 misses from the Dutch sees Morocco reach the Round of 16, where they will face co-hosts Canada. Earlier, one goal each from Cody Gakpo and Isa Diop, saw the game go into Extra Time.

Paraguay's finest hour: Giants Germany sent packing in Round of 32 Paraguay held Germany for 120 minutes before winning 4-3 in the shootout after a 1-1 draw. The result handed Germany their first World Cup shootout defeat and gave Paraguay a landmark victory.

Neymar hits back at German economist who predicted Japan would beat Brazil Neymar took a playful swipe at German economist Joachim Klement after Brazil's 2-1 win over Japan proved his World Cup prediction wrong, with the Brazil star's post quickly going viral.

Djokovic sees off Wu Yibing in Round 1 as proposal adds to Wimbledon spectacle Novak Djokovic overcame a spirited Wu Yibing in four sets to reach the Wimbledon second round, with a surprise marriage proposal in the stands and his witty request for a wedding invitation stealing attention.

Why did Neymar not play in the Round of 32 clash? Brazil vs Japan: Carlo Ancelotti revealed Neymar was kept on the bench against Japan as a contingency for extra time, with Brazil's tactical halftime switch inspiring a dramatic 2-1 comeback to reach the Round of 16.

Japan coach, players bow down to fans in apology as Brazil end World Cup dream FIFA World Cup 2026: Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu and his players bowed before their fans after Brazil's dramatic 2-1 win ended the Samurai Blue's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign in an emotional Round of 32 farewell.

West Indies have nothing to lose, will be fearless vs Australia: Hayley MatthewsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: West Indies skipper Hayley Matthews said that her team will be fearless when they face Sophie Molineux's Australia on Tuesday in the first semifinal at the Oval.

GER vs PAR Highlights: Paraguay does the unthinkable, knock out GermanyGermany vs Paraguay Highlights FIFA World Cup 2026: (GER 1 (3) - 1(4) PAR) Paraguay have stunned the four-time world champions, knocking them out of the FIFA World Cup. After, the game remained level by the end of extra-time, Paraguay toppled the German giants over penalties.

Don Carlo's big-game magic sends Brazil past brave Japan into Round of 16FIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil came from behind to beat a brave and well-organised Japan side 2-1 in Houston, sealing their place in the World Cup Round of 16. Gabriel Martinelli struck a dramatic stoppage-time winner after Casemiro had earlier cancelled out Kaishu Sano's first-half opener.

Australia wary of Hayley Matthews, Deandra Dottin threats in semis vs West IndiesWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: Ash Gardner said that Australia need to be careful with West Indies having match-winners like Deandra Dottin and Hayley Matthews going into the semis on Tuesday at the Oval.

Jannik Sinner explains playing with blood on shoe after nasty fall at WimbledonJannik Sinner brushed off concerns after playing through a bleeding toe to stage a remarkable five-set comeback against Miomir Kecmanovic in the Wimbledon 2026.

Saudi football's big ambitions hit wall as FA president Al-Misehal resigns after WC exitFIFA World Cup 2026: Saudi Arabia's World Cup exit has cost Yasser Al-Misehal his job after seven years in charge. The resignation exposes fresh questions over a lavish football project still short on tournament substance.

Jannik Sinner survives huge 5-set scare, battles injury to reach Round 2Jannik Sinner battled through a gruelling 3-hour-30-minute five-set win over Miomir Kecmanovic at Wimbledon 2026, overcoming an injury scare and a tiebreak setback to advance to Round 2.

BRA 2-1 JPN Highlights: Late Martinelli goal sends Brazil through to RO16Brazil (BRA) vs Japan (JPN) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (BRA 2-1 JPN) Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 in a thrilling Round of 32 clash. Casemiro cancelled out Japan's opener before Gabriel Martinelli struck deep into stoppage time to send the Selecao into the Round of 16.

No Alcaraz, no free pass: Vijay Amritraj sees tough Wimbledon for Jannik SinnerDespite Carlos Alcaraz's injury-enforced absence, Vijay Amritraj believes defending champion Jannik Sinner will face a stern test in his bid to retain the Wimbledon title, insisting there are no free passes at the All England Club.

How Australia flipped the script to send India crashing out: Ash Gardner revealsWomen's T20 World Cup: Ash Gardner revealed Australia overcame the pressure of a steep chase by targeting favourable match-ups, breaking the innings into smaller phases and backing their batting depth to attack India's bowlers.

Maja Chwalinska goes from French Open fairytale to Wimbledon heartbreakMaja Chwalinska's Wimbledon campaign ended in heartbreaking fashion after the Polish star squandered a set, a 5-2 lead and a match point before losing to Mananchaya Sawangkaew in the first round.

India vs Pakistan unlikely at 2028 Olympics. Cricket qualification rules explainedCricket's LA28 qualification rules have placed India firmly on track for Los Angeles while pushing Pakistan towards a treacherous global qualifier. By heavily prioritising regional rankings, the newly unveiled roadmap drastically raises the stakes for every bilateral series—leaving a blockbuster India-Pakistan Olympic clash far from guaranteed.

Day 1 Highlights: Djokovic overcomes Yibing, Mirra Andreeva beats Magda LinetteWimbledon 2026, Day 1 Highlights: Jannik Sinner has beaten Miomir Kecmanovic in five sets on Centre Court and advanced to the second round. Aryna Sabalenka defeated Teodora Kostovic in the first round. Later, Novak Djokovic beat China's Wu Yibing.

Iceland Cricket trolls Gambhir after India's historic series loss vs IrelandIndia's loss to Ireland triggered anger amongst cricket fans. The Gautam Gambhir-coached team faltered against the European side for the first time in the history of the game.

India women's cricket team qualifies for Olympics despite early World Cup exitIndia Women qualified for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics despite their group-stage exit from the ICC Women's T20 World Cup, earning Asia's lone automatic berth under the ICC's newly announced qualification pathway.

Ireland coach Heinrich Malan steps down a day after historic India series winHeinrich Malan stepped down as Ireland men's coach a day after a 2-0 T20I series sweep over India in Belfast. The move gives Cricket Ireland time to prepare for the next 50-over World Cup cycle.

After 3-day search through earthquake rubble, footballer finds family deadArgentine footballer Lucas Trejo spent three days searching through the rubble of his family home in Venezuela after last week's earthquakes. Authorities later recovered the bodies of his wife and two children.

I am there: Srikanth confident of brighter days after US Open runner-up finishKidambi Srikanth finished runner-up at the US Open after a hard-fought three-game final against Su Li-yang. The run strengthened his belief that his game is close to breaking through again.

Brazil vs Japan: FIFA World Cup prepares for potential classicBrazil return to action in the FIFA World Cup, as the 5-time champions take on Asia's best football team Japan. Will Brazil's Samba outmatch the underdogs, or will the Samurai cut down the Brazilian flair?

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
